papers.title: Judge, Then Drive: A Critic-Centric Vision Language Action Framework for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract_snippet: Recent advances in vision language action (VLA) models have shown remarkable potential for autonomous driving by directly mapping multimodal inputs to control signals. However, previous VLA-based methods have not explicitly exploited the critic capability of VLAs to refine driving decisions, even though such capability has been well demonstrated in other LLM-based domains, thereby limiting their performance in complex closed-loop scenarios. papers.reason: 聚焦自动驾驶（Autonomous Driving），非机器人操作/Embodied AI核心场景，属于过滤范围。

papers.title: World2Minecraft: Occupancy-Driven Simulated Scenes Construction
papers.abstract_snippet: Embodied intelligence requires high-fidelity simulation environments to support perception and decision-making, yet existing platforms often suffer from data contamination and limited flexibility. To mitigate this, we propose World2Minecraft to convert real-world scenes into structured Minecraft environments based on 3D semantic occupancy prediction. In the reconstructed scenes, we can effortlessly perform downstream tasks such as Vision-Language Navigation(VLN). However, we observe that reconst
papers.reason: 将真实世界场景转换为Minecraft仿真环境，虽有助于具身智能数据生成，但缺乏直接VLA训练或策略学习实验，属工具类工作。

papers.title: Robot Learning from Human Videos: A Survey
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.27621v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A critical bottleneck hindering further advancement in embodied AI and robotics is the challenge of scaling robot data. papers.reason: 综述论文，总结从人类视频学习机器人技能的研究进展，重要但不紧急，无新算法贡献。

papers.title: MotuBrain: An Advanced World Action Model for Robot Control
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.27792v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve strong semantic generalization but often lack fine-grained modeling of world dynamics. Recent work explores video generation models as a foundation for world modeling, leading to unified World Action Models (WAMs) that jointly model visual dynamics and actions.
